$comment: Real output from: node client/exec-bash.ts "curl https://api.example.com"
$why_this_matters: Mid-tier and small models hallucinate `curl` because it's the most common HTTP-fetching command in their training data. just-bash's sandbox doesn't include curl by default. Without a diagnostic, the model often retries the same approach with different URLs. With the diagnostic, it sees the actual catalog and switches to a registry skill (typically `ip-info` or `url2md`) on the next round.

diagnostics: One of the commands does not exist. Available registry commands: dictionary, echo-pretty, github-repo-info, ip-info, url2md, weather. Standard tools: jq, grep, sed, awk, xargs, head, wc, tr.

$delta: Raw output gives no signal about what IS available. Smart enumerates the full catalog so the model can pick a real alternative on the next round. Observed in Granite 4.0 H Micro Q3 composable: pivoted to ip-info on round 2 instead of trying another curl variant.
